summary: The IDF destroyed four Hamas rocket launch positions in Gaza during the cease‑fire, while Israeli forces reported a soldier injured by an explosion in Lebanon and two civilians wounded by an Israeli drone strike in Al‑Dabsha. The US embassy in Baghdad warned that Iran‑backed militias continue missile launches from Iraq, and President Trump announced that U.S. inspectors will be allowed to access Iranian uranium sites. Diplomatic activity includes US‑UAE talks on the Iran agreement and statements from Iran denying any tolls or fees on Hormuz shipping.
key_points: The IDF destroyed four Hamas rocket launch positions in Gaza during the cease‑fire (Source: @idf_telegram/24429).
An Israeli soldier was injured by an explosion in Lebanon; the incident is under investigation (Source: @naya_foriraq/79797).
Two civilians were injured by an Israeli drone strike in Al‑Dabsha, southern Lebanon, despite the cease‑fire (Source: @presstv/195434).
The US embassy in Baghdad said Iran‑backed militias in Iraq keep firing rockets and missiles (Source: @naya_foriraq/79789).
President Trump announced that U.S. inspectors will be permitted to access Iranian uranium sites (Source: @naya_foriraq/79786).
Israeli forces detained an unarmed suspected Hamas operative at the Kerem Shalom crossing (Source: @manniefabian/58668).
Hezbollah is reported to have built a network of tunnels, weapons depots, and missile sites in southern Lebanon with Iranian financial support (Source: @kianmeli1/88137).
US officials discussed the Iran‑U.S. memorandum with UAE leaders in Abu Dhabi (Source: @VahidOnline/76650).

sections.executive_summary: The IDF eliminated four Hamas rocket launch sites in Gaza during the cease‑fire, marking the most significant recent combat action. Simultaneously, Israel reported injuries in Lebanon from an explosion and a drone strike, indicating the cease‑fire’s fragility. Diplomatic pressure escalates as the US announces inspector access to Iranian nuclear sites and warns of continued Iranian‑backed militia activity from Iraq.

points: President Trump declared that U.S. inspectors will be allowed to access Iranian uranium facilities, signaling a potential shift in nuclear oversight (Source: @naya_foriraq/79786).
Trump also warned that negotiations would be halted immediately if Iran were found charging tolls or insurance fees on ships in the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @amitsegal/62500).
Iranian officials, as reported by US sources, claim no tolls or insurance costs are being sought from Hormuz shipping (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4249).
The US embassy in Baghdad warned that Iran‑backed militias in Iraq continue launching rockets and missiles, sustaining regional hostilities (Source: @naya_foriraq/79789).
Iran’s parliament speaker returned to Tehran after a trip to Baku, underscoring ongoing diplomatic engagements (Source: @presstv/195430).

The IDF reported destroying four Hamas rocket launch positions in Gaza that were set up during the cease‑fire, describing them as immediate threats to Israeli forces and civilians (Source: @idf_telegram/24429).
Israeli forces detained an unarmed suspected Hamas operative at the Kerem Shalom crossing after a security alert, emphasizing no infiltration into Israeli territory (Source: @manniefabian/58668).
IDF units moved to the Kerem Shalom area on suspicion of a Hamas operative, arresting one unarmed suspect for further questioning (Source: @amitsegal/62499).
A senior Israeli soldier was injured by an explosion in Lebanon; authorities are investigating the cause (Source: @naya_foriraq/79797).
The UN Commission of Inquiry condemned Israel for alleged systematic killing of children in Gaza and the occupied West Bank (Source: @presstv/195411).
During the night of the cease‑fire, the IDF destroyed four Hamas rocket launch sites across multiple locations in the Gaza Strip (Source: @yediotnews25/131864).
UN officials accused Israel of widespread and systematic killing of children in Gaza and the occupied West Bank (Source: @presstv/195411).
No further operational details were provided in the source messages regarding West Bank incidents.
No additional Gaza‑specific combat actions were reported beyond the rocket‑site destructions.
An Israeli soldier was injured by an explosion in Lebanon; the incident remains under investigation (Source: @naya_foriraq/79797).
Two civilians were wounded by an Israeli drone strike targeting a civilian vehicle in Al‑Dabsha, southern Lebanon, despite the cease‑fire (Source: @presstv/195434).

Hezbollah is said to have constructed an extensive network of tunnels, weapons depots, missile sites, and command centers in southern Lebanon with Iranian financial backing (Source: @kianmeli1/88137).
Audio reports indicated the sound of warplanes withdrawing from the Bekaa region in Lebanon (Source: @redlinkleb/28039).
The US embassy in Baghdad highlighted that Iran‑backed militias in Iraq continue to launch rockets and missiles, sustaining regional hostilities (Source: @naya_foriraq/79789).
A senior ISIS commander, Hussein al‑Olivi, was reportedly killed by a US strike in northern Syria (Source: @yediotnews25/131878).
No further operational details about Syria or Iraq were provided in the source messages.
US Secretary of State Antony Blinken met with UAE leaders in Abu Dhabi to discuss the US‑Iran memorandum and ensure safe passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @VahidOnline/76650).
No additional Gulf‑state specific combat actions were reported in the source messages.

key_developments: IDF destroyed four Hamas rocket launch positions in Gaza during the cease‑fire, removing immediate threats to Israeli forces (Source: @idf_telegram/24429).
An Israeli soldier was injured by an explosion in Lebanon, highlighting ongoing cross‑border volatility (Source: @naya_foriraq/79797).
Two civilians were wounded by an Israeli drone strike in Al‑Dabsha, southern Lebanon, despite the cease‑fire (Source: @presstv/195434).
US embassy in Baghdad warned that Iran‑backed militias in Iraq continue missile launches, sustaining regional conflict (Source: @naya_foriraq/79789).
President Trump announced U.S. inspectors will be allowed to access Iranian uranium sites, indicating a potential shift in nuclear oversight (Source: @naya_foriraq/79786).
Hezbollah’s extensive tunnel and weapons network in southern Lebanon, funded by Iran, poses a strategic threat to Israel (Source: @kianmeli1/88137).
US‑UAE talks in Abu Dhabi focused on the Iran‑U.S. memorandum and safe navigation of the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @VahidOnline/76650).
sections.threat_assessment: Iran‑backed militias in Iraq remain active, launching rockets that threaten both Israeli and regional assets. Hezbollah's fortified tunnel and missile infrastructure in southern Lebanon, supported by Iranian financing, sustains a high‑risk front against Israel. The IDF's recent destruction of Hamas launch sites in Gaza shows continued Israeli offensive capability, while civilian casualties from Israeli strikes in Lebanon risk further escalation.
sections.regional_response: The United States is increasing diplomatic pressure, offering inspector access to Iranian nuclear sites and engaging Gulf partners such as the UAE to secure maritime routes. Israel continues targeted operations in Gaza and heightened security measures along the Lebanese border. Iran denies any financial burdens on Hormuz shipping, maintaining its strategic posture while supporting proxy forces.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Iranian militia rocket launches from Iraq should be intensified, with satellite and SIGINT assets focused on launch sites. Hezbollah's tunnel network warrants ground‑penetrating radar surveys and HUMINT infiltration to assess operational readiness. The impact of US inspector access on Iran's nuclear timeline must be evaluated for potential shifts in Tehran's strategic calculations.
